The Official BNI Podcast

A weekly audio discussion with Dr. Ivan Misner, the Founder and Chief Visionary Officer of BNI, the world's largest business networking organization.

Special Episode 1: BNI online™

March 16, 2020 by Ivan Misner 55 Comments

Podcast: Download

Subscribe: Apple Podcasts | RSS | Subscription Options

Print This Episode
This entry is part 1 of 4 in the series Special Episodes

Hi, this is Ivan Misner, Founder and Chief Visionary Officer of BNI. This is the first ever Special Episode of the BNI Podcast. Special Episode Number One. Please make sure to tell your fellow BNI members about this episode. It’s an important one.

Necessity is the mother of invention. Thirty-five years ago, I was going through a pretty tough business challenge. The result of that challenge was the creation of BNI.

Many of us are experiencing some pretty difficult challenges today. Once again, I believe that necessity is the mother of invention.

There is no higher priority than the safety and well-being of our members and leaders in BNI.

We have been closely monitoring world events and preparing the organization to continue to provide support to you and to your business. We have committed significant resources and are investing in new tools to support you during this time.

As a result, we have created a powerful new meeting platform called BNI online™. It’s a simple and effective way for you to stay connected to your network from the comfort of your home or office. It’s essentially the same as a traditional BNI meeting with the same structure and agenda, and all it takes is a few mouse clicks.

We have successfully run trials of the BNI online™ platform and it has proven to be effective in generating quality referrals and “Thank You for Closed Business.”

Your Chapter President or BNI Director will be reaching out to you in the coming days with more information about this great new service that we have prepared to help you during this time.

The strength of this organization has always been in the way members come together to help and support each other. I have seen this first-hand over the years. I’ve seen how members have come together after the earthquake in Northridge, California, the hurricanes like Katrina, the Tsunami in Japan, and the recent brush fires in Australia. When members share their needs with other members, I have seen magic happen. Over and over again, I have watched as members of this organization support one another in a way that I find both incredible and humbling.

We have a chance to do this again today. I am so proud to see the members, directors, and global support team step up to find solutions to the current problem.

Our mission, in BNI, remains the same: To help members increase their business through a structured, positive, and professional referral marketing program.

The mission remains the same. Only the mechanism has changed. When the time is right, we can go back to our face-to-face meetings. But we will go back to them with a powerful awareness of what is possible for us in the future.

When I was diagnosed with cancer eight years ago, the very first thing I did was write down a list of the Possible Positive Outcomes of this very serious situation. I came up with a list of many items. I have to tell you that every single one of those potential outcomes has come true for me. Every one.

I ask you to do the same. Make a list of all the Possible Positive Outcomes that this interim transition can have for you.

Here are four to help you get started:

  1. Others may struggle during this time, but you’ll be in a position to continue to do referral business every week.
  2. If there’s something you need, you’ll have access to your referral partners to help you with that need.
  3. Your commute to your BNI meeting is as far away as your computer.
  4. Hey, you can attend a BNI meeting in your sweatpants and no one will ever know.

Take a few moments and create your own list of Possible Positive Outcomes that can occur over the next few weeks. I bet you will be able to achieve most, or all of them.

This is an opportunity to keep your business going when others don’t have access to the powerful network that you do.

We’re going to come out the other side of this and we’ll be a stronger organization when we do. We will be stronger because we are all part of a community that shares a powerful core value. And that core value is our philosophy of Givers Gain. Today more than ever, we need to support each other and BNI online™ is a tool to help us do that.

To me, BNI is more than a meeting, it’s been a movement. It’s about Changing the Way the World Does Business.

I invite you to share your stories here on this site. Tell me how you’re using bni online™ to help you weather this difficult time. I want to hear your story.

Stay healthy and we’ll help you stay successful.

This is Ivan Misner, Founder of BNI. Thank you.

ShareTweetLinkedinWhatsappSaveSendFlipboard

Part of the series Special Episodes Posted in Announcements, Online Networking Tagged with BNI online™

Comments

    Solutions-focused comments are welcome. Dr. Misner reads every comment personally.

    All comments are moderated before posting. Profanity, personal attacks, and sales pitches will be deleted.

    March 16, 2020 at 9:01 am

    Safety is better than sorry

    Reply

    • Ivan Misner says

      March 16, 2020 at 10:52 am

      True. Graham Weihmiller, BNI’s CEO, did an amazing job of getting this out to the BNI members.

      Reply

      • Sandeep Jain says

        March 17, 2020 at 1:17 am

        Amazing thank you so much Sir

  2. Itzik Vilc says

    March 16, 2020 at 9:35 am

    Dear Ivan,

    Thank you for your strong leadership in these unsure times.

    So happy to be a BNI member and also an DC and help my chapters.

    Reply

    • Karthick Jayachandran says

      April 5, 2020 at 7:47 am

      Can we record our online 121 in BNI connect as we are in lockdown ?

      Reply

      • Ivan Misner says

        April 10, 2020 at 12:59 pm

        A legitimate 1-2-1? I don’t see why not.

        Ivan

  3. Ivan Misner says

    March 16, 2020 at 10:52 am

    Thanks Itzik. Graham Weihmiller (CEO) takes most of the credit.

    Reply

  4. Tara Rising says

    March 16, 2020 at 12:06 pm

    Thank you for your leadership and encouragement! I’m excited that we can keep giving referrals and growing our own businesses through BNI Online!!!

    Reply

    • Ivan Misner says

      March 16, 2020 at 3:57 pm

      Thank you Tara.

      Reply

  5. Mark Seery says

    March 16, 2020 at 12:47 pm

    Dear Ivan,

    Thank you. I could not be prouder of the Leadership of BNI in preparing, responding and commitment to our Members, Leaders and Communities both local and Global during this temporary, yet challenging.

    As you have demonstrated time and time again, some of the attributes of great leadership are candor, commitment, courage, communication, compassion, decisiveness, action and maintaining a personal and professional mindset of Givers Gain. This is what we are experiencing from BNI Global and what we will emulate throughout.

    Reply

    • Ivan Misner says

      March 16, 2020 at 3:58 pm

      Thanks Mark. I truly appreciate your kind words.

      Ivan

      Reply

  6. Jeannemarie Viggiano says

    March 16, 2020 at 3:50 pm

    Thank you for keeping the ship moving forward! I love that we are going to keep meeting online! I am also going to set up some one-to-one virtually too.
    I think it’s a great opportunity to work on some areas of my business that need freshening up!

    Reply

  7. Bernard TANG says

    March 16, 2020 at 4:34 pm

    I am not sure what will happen when writing a positive possible opportunities list. I believe BNI is able to do changing the way to do business. I will share this message to my chapter and encourage people in my room writing a positive possible opportunities list. do something is better than sitting in front of computer. thanks for Dr. Ivan Misner.

    Reply

  8. Srinivas Pyla says

    March 16, 2020 at 7:56 pm

    Adaptability is the biggest advantage in BNI,
    Thank you.

    Reply

  9. Héctor Lizcano Gama says

    March 16, 2020 at 8:22 pm

    Muy bien, muchas gracias, pienso que la reunion cara a cara es fundamental para ayudar a el compromiso con cada uno de los compañeros, ojalá esto pase pronto, saludos del capitulo Morelia Empresarial, en Morelia Michoacán México.

    Reply

  10. Ivan J Gonzalez says

    March 16, 2020 at 9:26 pm

    Long ago I learned the 10/90 rule when facing adversity – 10% attributable to the event while 90% of the outcome attributable to how one reacts to it. This event will have short-term hick-up on our business but taking advantage of current technology to continue the Givers Gain philosophy at BNI will have a long-lasting positive impact on most of us. Congratulations on a swift response. Stay the course and stay safe — the coming weeks will be the key to controlling this pandemic and BNI online will enable us to continue to build our business.

    Reply

    • Ivan Misner says

      March 17, 2020 at 9:47 am

      Well said Ivan Gonzalez!

      Reply

  11. James stilloe says

    March 16, 2020 at 9:40 pm

    BNI will lead the way during these new and unpredictable times and as always givers gain and the proof is in the members that will rally together to make it happen . Thank you Dr. Misner .

    Reply

    • Ivan Misner says

      March 17, 2020 at 9:54 am

      Thanks James, it’s certainly interesting times and I want our members to be able to get through this positioned to grow when this is all over.

      Reply

  12. Dr.Anil Sharma says

    March 16, 2020 at 11:45 pm

    Hi Ivan
    Thanks for creating such a platform where everyone is becoming an innovator irrespective of age and profession . Learning to fight unsure problems. Great and thanks once again

    Reply

    • Dhammika Jayasuriya says

      March 18, 2020 at 9:33 am

      HI Ivan.
      Excellent initiative Thank you for feeling the Feelings of young chapters and creativity of a nucleus medium.

      Reply

  13. SP Tiwari says

    March 17, 2020 at 1:11 am

    One of the Core Value of BNI is Innovation. Keeping in mind the Traditional Philosophy of Givers Gain, BNI has innovated to BNI Online. Truly Remarkable Leadership. Proud to be a BNI Member !!

    Reply

  14. A.Muralidhar says

    March 17, 2020 at 3:13 am

    Yes, thanks to BNI, EDs, LT and Online Hosts … While others are talking about crisis, we managed to have the meeting today started at 8AM sharp and ended at 9.30 AM Sharp passed refferal, money slips and Testimonials

    Reply

  15. Edson M . de Moraes says

    March 17, 2020 at 7:53 am

    Estamos passando por momento de mudanças e comportamentos de nós seres humanos, em relação ao que esta acontencendo no MUNDO .BNI . tem papel importante PARA FAZERMOS HISTORIA.
    Força ao BNI -online.
    Obrigado Dr Ivan

    Reply

  16. James McConnell says

    March 17, 2020 at 8:35 am

    Hi Ivan,
    This is exactly what I expected from BNI Leadership. I am very excited and proud to belong to such a great network of business professionals. Thank you to everyone that has made this possible as we journey on together in a very complicated situatuion.

    Reply

  17. Ashwani says

    March 17, 2020 at 8:52 am

    Dear Mr Ivan
    Good morning BNI.
    BNI Online is one of the most innovative solutions for online meeting , we have seen in recent times.
    Proud to be associated with.

    Reply

  18. Roji Thomas says

    March 18, 2020 at 12:02 am

    Hello Dr. Ivan Miser,

    Hope you are keeping well. Thank You Very Much for your visionary leadership to BNI.

    We had an excellent first BNI Online meeting today morning.. Appreciate all the Global Leadership Team and technology partners for such a swift change in the need of the hour.

    It was a great meeting.

    Kind Regards
    Roji Thomas
    [email protected]
    https://bni-cochin.in/cochin-achievers/en-IN/index

    Reply

  19. Moses says

    March 18, 2020 at 3:59 am

    Oh . This is food for thought… As for me, the number one thing on my mind always is my healthy. Nothing can real happen on this planet earth when ones healthy is in shambles. Every one must practice healthy fit(NESS) before positive living in adversity comes into the PICTURE.

    Reply

  20. Jonathan Corbin says

    March 18, 2020 at 8:43 am

    Ivan,
    Thank you for the direction you give. I love your the idea of creating a list of possible positive outcomes from a problem/challenge. This is definitely something I will share with others. Its such a great way to set a positive direction when we don’t feel positive about the current situation!

    Thanks you so much!

    Reply

  21. Carl Knows Wine says

    March 18, 2020 at 9:09 am

    What a fabulous initiative. I joined in January and have found that my involvement with my BNI Chapter – BNI West Island Professionals here in the Montreal area has really helped me to focus on growing my business. The weekly meetings are key to that motivation and I was concerned about what might happen if we couldn’t meet.

    Yesterday we had an impromptu Zoom meeting with a good portion of the membership who were available and it went well. I think we are all ready for our first official BNI Online meeting next week. Our leadership team is getting training on it as we speak and will get the details out to the membership soon.

    Thanks to all the BNI team who have put this together

    Reply

  22. Carl Bird says

    March 18, 2020 at 10:51 am

    I wanted to add might (so far) short list:

    1. Take more time to catch up with recent connections where follow up is needed
    2. Establish a regular blog for my business (I am a consulting sommelier and wine educator)
    3. Better define my “Why”
    4. Work on the third part of my 12-12-12 – that is the first 12 words I use to tell someone what I do when I first meet them.
    5. Create a workable solution to provide easy to understand webinars to help wine lovers demystify the complex world of wine, which can be intimidating.

    That’s it for now but I am sure things will be added as this situation continues

    Reply

    • Ivan Misner says

      March 18, 2020 at 11:07 am

      Carl, that is a really good list. Well done!

      Thanks for sharing it.

      Reply

  23. Sandra says

    March 18, 2020 at 2:33 pm

    Health & Safety is th main focus for us all!
    Love the BNI Online meeting idea. Keep momentum going until we can all meet again.
    This will pass eventually.
    We must stay positive and practice positivity.
    Help one another in times of need.
    Talk to one another for comfort.
    Stay connected to one another through the amazing use of technology. No one needs to be alone in this time of adversity.
    Love and be kind to one another.

    The list of ideas one of the members (Carl Bird) posted in comments are very helpful for us to keep our focus. Great ideas!

    Reply

  24. Kei Sato says

    March 19, 2020 at 12:06 am

    Hi.
    I am from Liberta chapter in Meguro in Japan.
    Your podcast always inspires me.
    I am Kei Sato promoting English education in Japan.
    Actually, online meetings are not the same as the meeting in person but I believe, especially in business regardless of being a BNI member or not, that we should make best use of this kind of situation.
    Whatever may happen, we should act positively.
    The stable mind leads us to the better world.
    I also appreciate this situation, I mean online meeting because many of my business partners have been forced to stop working. But we, BNI members can share some information like this.
    I think BNI has full of possibilities to promote some new ways of working.

    Reply

  25. Ray Hasto says

    March 19, 2020 at 5:30 am

    Ivan, thank you for the thoughts and leadership. Every challenge presents opportunity. While speaking with 2 clients yesterday, I had the chance to ask what else they need, since I’m in contact with other professionals I trust. A simple question that generated referrals to two of my fellow BNI members. Times with obvious pressure are presenting business owners with the understanding that things they’ve put off really DO need to be addressed. Thanks to BNI, we have the connections to help!

    Reply

    • Ivan Misner says

      March 19, 2020 at 10:37 am

      That’s a great example of using your network. Thanks. Ray.

      Reply

  26. Arvind Nanda says

    March 19, 2020 at 10:05 am

    Hello Dr Misner,

    The session was really well sought after and the need of the hour. Members are not only getting ready for a new phase in upgrading themselves with BNI Online but also understanding now the meaning of our
    BNI Core Value – *Tradition and Innovation*

    Your phrase- “I hate change but even more to fail” sums it all up.

    Thank you,

    Arvind Nanda
    BNI Champions
    Goa
    India

    Reply

  27. Eva Menon says

    March 19, 2020 at 12:55 pm

    I am working on on my business online. This has given me the time and opportunity to be more educated on online platforms. In 1 week I have increased my online viewership and my sales by about 75%. I have been consistent for the past 1.5 years with not too many results. Now all of a sudden, I have gotten 3 orders from folks I do not know, they found me online, I am able to send out positivity in the world and I can meet with several BNI members in one day because I am not driving. I think if we stay positive and work on ourselves we will come out of this stronger than before.

    Reply

  28. George J Simons says

    March 20, 2020 at 1:33 pm

    Ivan,

    If there was ever a time to “Dig Deep” and really understand the personal side of all members, the time is now. There will be members down and out. They see their dreams of a small business eroding and looking for ideas and help. I encourage all BNI members worldwide, to stay in touch will members outside of meetings and let them know our whole organization is there to listen and offer help and ideas going forward. Now is the time to be a real BNI leader and true friend. Ivan, you have taught us all to be prepared for this moment. Look forward to meeting you someday to give you a personal Thank You>

    Sincerely,

    George J. Simons
    Prosperity Alliance of Richmond, Va

    Reply

  29. Bernie Kennedy says

    March 23, 2020 at 1:12 pm

    The power of BNI. During an online meeting the other day initiated by another member of our chapter, one of our members shared that she was in distress because of a plumbing issue on an upstairs floor, now coming down the walls. Within minutes 3 of our members stepped up to deliver a dehumidifier, giant fan and other tools needed. All would be at your home within the next few hours. The power of “givers gain” was evident!

    Reply

  30. Adrian Jimenez says

    March 24, 2020 at 3:28 pm

    Excelente inciativa, vamos a trabajar para mejorar nuestras referencias y ayudar a nuestros socios.

    Reply

  31. Sheri says

    March 26, 2020 at 10:10 am

    The opportunity to meet online is one answer to the current times. Yes it is a way to stay connected. Although there’s almost too much online time :) Tho with many of us in 30 day at home orders, the money is not flowing in as well, despite connections. Yet, the monthly fee is the same despite suspension of rent and breakfast/lunch. Perhaps taking a salary cut throughout all management and fee reduction or even a 30 day hold on our fees would also keep members motivated to stay in during this crucial time of crisis. It’s both business and needing people to pay for the business referred. Just a thought.

    Reply

    • Ivan Misner says

      March 26, 2020 at 12:19 pm

      Sheri, you’re right about online time. It’s important to be selective about online time. Limit the funny cat videos and focus on pouring into yourself with education and motivation. Connect with people in virtual 1-2-1’s. That is productive online time.

      Regarding BNI, this is definitely impacting us as an organization. We invested massive resources to transition BNI to an online platform (think of 9,500 Zoom accounts for all our groups, the training, and staffing resources to create manuals and processes in just a matter of weeks). We have invested heavily to make sure members can continue to benefit from the participation. Second, we unfortunately had to lay off 1/3 of our global staff (at least on an interim basis). In addition, all remaining BNI employees have taken an across the board 25% pay cut until further notice. Graham Weihmiller (CEO) and I have taken a 50% pay cut.

      BNI is a face-to-face networking organization. Our entire business was face-to-face. We are trying to turn-on-a-dime to continue offering services to our members in an online platform. We feel the squeeze to the extreme.

      A message will go out soon as to what we can do for the members AND still stay in business ourselves. Be patient and be understanding. We are all in this together. All of us.

      Reply

  32. Eric Dutton says

    March 26, 2020 at 12:00 pm

    Hello, Ivan.
    Thank you for making this happen.
    Could this platform be made available for power team meetings?

    Reply

    • Ivan Misner says

      March 26, 2020 at 12:56 pm

      Talk to your Executive Director. I “think” it is possible.

      Reply

      • Carl Bird says

        March 26, 2020 at 2:04 pm

        According to our Leadership team, we will be scheduling Power Team meetings via Zoom very soon.

  33. Mihir Parmar says

    March 31, 2020 at 12:45 pm

    Sir, Does our BNI online allow us to attend meetings of other regions /countries as observers freely?

    Appreciate your response on this.

    Reply

    • Ivan Misner says

      April 10, 2020 at 1:00 pm

      Most likely but it can be abused. Talk to your local BNI Director.

      Reply

  34. Francisco Luna says

    April 1, 2020 at 9:26 pm

    Hola Ivan

    Existe un antidoto para la incertidumbre….. es la accion y la colaboracion
    hoy nos llegan noticias desalentadoras y retadoras a la vez
    por que un buen empresario siempre estará actuando ante la adversidad

    Gracias por tu guia y motivacion

    dicen en mi Pais

    NO HAY PEOR COSA … QUE LA QUE NO SE HAGA

    Vamos seamos creativos y enfoquemos toda nuestra energía en HACER

    Saludos

    Francisco Luna
    Pisos Industriales
    Es tiempo de dar

    Reply

  35. Rahul Agarwal says

    April 2, 2020 at 12:00 am

    Thanks Ivan. Your positive energy as always ensures we also carry on the same energy to our chapter/members.

    Reply

  36. Gina Buchanan says

    September 23, 2020 at 1:39 pm

    Dr. Misner thank you so much for these Special podcasts. As a Virtual Assistant, I always try to prepare my clients, partnerships, and friends for an online environment. Now that we are well into this pandemic I prepare everyone even more because everything around us is going to view things differently and being ready for anything is key.

    Reply

  37. S.Manoj babu says

    June 20, 2021 at 6:18 am

    Dear Sir,

    After reading initially this episode, the only Possible positive outcomes felt was this meeting should happen continuously and my friends should not leave, after 6 months from april 2020 of the pandemic, we saw us going good. Now we are used and somebody said, that there is only difference in the spelling of ONLINE and OFFLINE, rest remains same. Its true.
    Especially our weekly training programs from our ED – Mr.Sunil Setia – Chennai CBD B region is so excellent and he participates often in our chapter meetings and other zoom meetings. Again we get more educated from your PODCAST, now we have a program called “” Did you have your (knowledge) Breakfast” (This means – did you listen minimum one podcast before your food Breakfast) .This has helped us to move forward without deviation and together.

    Also during this year 20-21, on my personal side I have grown 25% in business compared to last year.

    I dedicate this success to BNI

    BNI AZURE
    Chennai CBD-B region
    Tamil Nadu – India

    Reply

  38. Eyeedul Haque says

    July 15, 2021 at 8:14 am

    Dear Ivan,

    Thanks for this very important message. As we emerge at the other end of the pandemic in the UK, I am glad to say that I was a member of BNI Wimbledon, and also the wider BNI group.

    I know that people often say that we are in the same boat, or that we are in this together. In reality, some businesses or occupations fared better than others over the last 12 months. And sadly, there have been many casualties in terms of businesses closing their doors.

    But by continuing with BNI, and moving chapter meetings to online, helped keep our members motivated and ensured that we were together. We did keep tabs on each other and help us through turbulent times. And many of us have made good friends through the trust that we have built with BNI members.

    On a personal note, I managed to attend meetings with members in Singapore and HK and have made good professional connections. And hopefully, as a property buying agent in London, we will be carrying out business with BNI members from SE Asia. The pandemic actually forged these relationships.

    Thanks for leading the organisation, as I know that you will have faced similar business interruptions over the years.

    Reply

  39. Col Virendra S Tavathia says

    May 8, 2022 at 8:03 pm

    After almost year and half being online Networking, we thought that it might be a good idea to meet in person. I personally feel that meeting in person makes a better sense, and gives a sense of belonging. Online Networking was good to fight the pandemic. But as we have now normal situation. We still follow precautions. One month in person meeting has resulted in adding two new members. While online networking resulted in loss of about 6 members. We are in the process of rebuilding. BNI support during the pandemic has been phenomenal. We want to thank everyone who helped us continue networking through the pandemic.

    Reply

  40. Sebastián Gracia says

    February 28, 2024 at 2:11 am

    This is powerful when you listen to it.
    When you listen to it twice is even more powerful.
    But when you listen to it three times, makes sense.
    Thank you Mr. Ivan for share your ideas to the world and have this vision of a community of givers that never stop working for make it grow. I’ve been a member since 2015 and I’m a proud member of course!!

    Reply

Products


  • Networking Is a Marathon, Not a Sprint     Learn how to Outlast, Outconnect, and Outperform the competition, through patience, consistency, and a giver’s mindset.

  • Infinite Legacy: The 7 Principles of Creating a Lasting Impact With Your Life

  • The 3rd Paradigm: A Radical Shift to Greater Success

  • Who's in Your Room? Revised and Updated: The Question That Will Change Your Life

  • The Connector Effect: The Proven Way to Grow Your Business Right Now

  • Infinite Giving: The 7 Principles of Givers Gain

  • Avoiding the Networking Disconnect

  • 29% Solution     It's not "net-sit" or "net-eat," it's "net-WORK"

  • Business Networking and Sex (Not What You Think)     It's no surprise that communicating with the opposite sex can be tricky. Finally, help is here.
